question: Why does Matthew include the ‘worship’ response to the risen Jesus (and no correction from Jesus), and how can you use that to answer ‘Jesus never accepted worship’?

choices.A: Matthew records worship directed to Jesus and presents it positively; Jesus does not rebuke it, supporting the claim that worship of Jesus is appropriate.
choices.B: Matthew condemns the worship as a sin.
choices.C: Jesus refuses worship and tells them to worship only the Father.
choices.D: The disciples worship the angels near the tomb, not Jesus.

teaching.title: Worship without rebuke
teaching.body: Matthew narrates worship offered to the risen Jesus and doesn’t depict Jesus correcting it. In a Jewish monotheistic context, that narrative choice matters: worship is not treated as misdirected. Apologetically, it challenges the claim that Jesus consistently rejected worship.
